preamble: The voiced velar fricative /ɣ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the voiced alveolar fricative /z/ through a process of fronting, where the point of articulation shifts from the velum to the alveolar ridge while maintaining its fricative manner and voicing.

examples.note: Attested in specific dialects such as Kea, Sikinos, Folegandros, Kalymnos, Nisyros, and Patmos.

sources: Manolessou, I., & Pantelidis, N. (2012). Velar fronting in Modern Greek dialects. Studies in Greek Linguistics, 32, 276-290.
